meta.note: Mock data — real tools were unavailable at scan time.

nuclei_findings.name: Exposed .git Configuration
nuclei_findings.description: The /.git/config file is publicly accessible. This can leak repository metadata, remote URLs, and branch names.
nuclei_findings.remediation: Block access to /.git/ via web-server configuration.

nuclei_findings.name: Missing Security Headers
nuclei_findings.description: The server response is missing several recommended HTTP security headers.
nuclei_findings.remediation: Add the missing headers in your web server or application middleware.

nuclei_findings.name: Open Redirect
nuclei_findings.description: A redirect parameter accepts arbitrary URLs, enabling phishing attacks.
nuclei_findings.remediation: Validate and allowlist redirect targets on the server side.

zap_findings.description: X-Frame-Options header is not included in the HTTP response to protect against Clickjacking attacks.
zap_findings.solution: Most modern Web servers can be configured to set this header.

zap_findings.description: A cookie has been set without the SameSite attribute, which means that the cookie can be sent as a result of a cross-site request.
zap_findings.solution: Ensure that the SameSite attribute is set to either 'lax' or ideally 'strict'.

zap_findings.alert: Server Leaks Version Information via 'Server' HTTP Response Header
zap_findings.description: The web/application server is leaking version information via the 'Server' HTTP response header. Access to such information may facilitate attackers identifying other vulnerabilities your version of software is subject to.
zap_findings.solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, etc. is configured to suppress the 'Server' header.
